Your Complete Prompt Library

© COPYRIGHT 2023 JON BENSON, DIGITAL PUBLISHER LLC   |   ALL RIGHTS RESERVED NO ELEMENTS OF THIS COURSE CAN BE TAUGHT WITHOUT EXPRESS WRITTEN PERMISSION FROM DIGITAL PUBLISHER







Note: All prompts must be used with the formulas and pre-prompts covered in the video training, or with existing ChatGPT copy, otherwise they cannot work. Each prompt is found within the specific training documents for any given section. This is just meant as a handy guide to find the prompts you need faster.



Buyer’s Roadmap Prompt

This uses SEARCH 1 as your pre-prompt to get the data for your Buyer’s Roadmap. Refer to the video training: Building Your Buyers Roadmap, Part 1.



> Act as if you are a professional direct response copywriter who loves to use visceral, real-life, dimensional language. Then complete SEARCH 1 using [Niche] and [Avatar] above as your foundation. 







Refiner Prompt

Make any text more dimensional and expressive. Dimensional copy is copy you can see and feel versus copy you simply “understand”. It’s far more persuasive.



> That’s good, but I can’t see and feel the results. Reword each using real-world examples and context while being as conversational as possible.







Branding Prompt

Create brand names for hooks, unique mechanisms, mistaken beliefs, and more. Simply change the text in blue to whatever you need branded. The last sentence is optional.



> I love what you wrote for mistaken beliefs. But can you come up with clever names for each mistaken belief? Use 4 words or less, and write in Title Case. Then tell me what the truth is for each mistaken belief.







Expansion Prompt

Write more for any given section. Simply change the text in blue to whatever you need.



> Thank you! But can you write about 200 words about each Mistaken Belief Truth? Please write each with dimensional language that uses real-world examples I can see and feel.







Rewrite Specific Sections

Simply change the text in blue to whatever you need



> Rewrite and rephrase the paragraphs after the last bullet point







Offer Branding Prompt

Used to create offer names, bonus and bonus ideas, demo testimonials, and more. 



> Act as if you are a professional direct response copywriter and write me 10 [Offer Name] examples, give me a price range for it, write a 30-day money back guarantee for it, come up with 3 complimentary [Bonus Name] and give each a retail value, then write a description for each that's so good people will buy my offer just for the bonuses. Then create 3 sample testimonials for my offer. Reference DATA above and draw on that.







Pattern Interrupt Prompt

Use in the Snap Suggestion segment of the VSL (Step 1) to create a pattern interrupt.



> Pretend you are a professional direct response copywriter. Create a video sales letter pattern interrupt using DATA as a reference for a weight loss offer. Do not use exclamation marks. Create it as if it were a scene from a movie, with a twist ending and a massive cliff-hanger.







Pattern Interrupt Refiner Prompt

Use this to refine your pattern interrupt if you want it more mysterious and if you need a pattern interrupt image to use later in your VSL (see training video).



> That's good, but do not mention that there is an offer or a plan or anything to buy. Make sure you also create something that can be used as an "anchor object" that I can refer back to later. Keep it mysterious.



Lead Prompt

Use in the Snap Suggestion segment of the VSL (Step 1) to create your VSL’s lead.



> Act as if you are a professional direct response copywriter who loves to use visceral, real-life, dimensional language. Then complete DATA above as your foundation. 







Refiner Prompt (More Juice!)

Refine your copy with more dimensional language.



> That’s good, but I can’t see and feel the results. Reword this entire section and create different goals and complaints. Use real-world examples and context while being as conversational as possible. Keep the language simple. Paint a vivid picture.







Create Returns Prompt

Get your copy more prepared for VSL slides by using this prompt.



> I love all of this, but I need you to create returns after each period and after each comma. Leave off the comma at the end of the return.







Different Goals Prompt

Get Chat to create a different set of goals for you so it’s less repetitive.



> You’re being too repetitive with these goals. Please create totally new goals that are inline with DATA and the rest of the content. Be sure to be dimensional. Paint a picture. Use real-world examples.







Get Specific Prompt

Get Chat to cite studies and facts to back up claims. NOTE: Always double-check Chat’s facts, as AI tends to lie sometimes.



> Expand on this by citing a study or facts.









Bullet Expander Prompt

Get Chat to write paragraphs from bullet points.



> That’s good, but I need you to flush out the ideas that currently read as bullet points. Take them and create full sentences and paragraphs. Use real-world examples and context while being as conversational as possible. Keep the language simple. Paint a vivid picture. Put a return after each period.
